architecture.summary: CE-Bench is a contrastive evaluation benchmark for assessing the interpretability of sparse autoencoders (SAEs) applied to large language models. The system uses a curated dataset of contrastive story pairs to measure whether SAE-learned features capture semantically meaningful distinctions. Evaluation flows from SAE-decomposed hidden states through contrastive scoring without requiring an external LLM judge.

biblio.title: CE-Bench: Towards a Reliable Contrastive Evaluation Benchmark of Interpretability of Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: CE-Bench achieves over 70% Spearman correlation with results in SAEBench
CE-Bench reliably measures the interpretability of sparse autoencoders
CE-Bench aligns well with existing benchmarks without requiring an external LLM judge
CE-Bench is a novel and lightweight contrastive evaluation benchmark for sparse autoencoders, built on a curated dataset of contrastive story pairs
